### Post by bombozz on 2010-08-19

My wired connection works fine. 

Any help would be appreciated.

### Post by grahammechanical on 2010-08-20
I also am new so I may not be able to help much. I do not understand what is going wrong. How do you know that your wireless connection is established? Can you left click on the network manager icon and does it list your ISP connection under Wireless Networks? Do you see a list of available wireless networks. Is your ISP among them? Have you selected it to connect to? 

If your wired connection works fine, as you say, then I am guessing that you cannot connect with wireless because you have (a) not selected a wireless network to connect to (network manager will ask for a password or wireless key - called WPA-PSK. (b) not set the connection to connect automatically. (c) not got user permissions to access the internet.

These are the simple things that I would check. I am not experienced enough to suggest more complicated solutions. Sorry.

Regards.

---

### Post by Iowan on 2010-08-20
Probably not the problem, but...
check **iptables -L** to see if/what rules are present.

### Post by bobcrunch on 2010-08-20
[SIZE=3]See my [SOLVED] post from 8/20 on wireless connection configuration using Ubuntu 10.04.  Just because it shows "Connection Established", doesn't mean you're connected.  There are other parameters that need defining.  Use "Edit Connection" as described.[/SIZE]

---
